Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product in the Tulunadu E-Store catalog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the product entity.

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit; a unique identifier for the product.

category.description: The main category of the product.

subCategory.description: A more specific sub-category of the product.

price.description: The regular price of the product.

salePrice.description: The discounted price of the product, if applicable.

currency.description: The currency in which the product is priced.

stockQty.description: The number of units of the product currently in stock.

stockStatus.description: The current stock status of the product (e.g., in stock, out of stock).

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

shortDescription.description: A brief description of the product.

images.description: An array of URLs pointing to product images.

tags.description: An array of tags associated with the product.

gstRate.description: The GST (Goods and Services Tax) rate applicable to the product.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents an order placed in the Tulunadu E-Store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the order entity.

orderId.description: The unique identifier for the order (external facing).

items.description: An array of items included in the order.

subtotal.description: The total price of the items in the order, before shipping and tax.

shipping.description: The shipping cost for the order.

tax.description: The tax amount for the order.

total.description: The total amount of the order, including items, shipping, and tax.

paymentStatus.description: The payment status of the order (e.g., pending, paid, failed, COD).

createdAt.description: The date and time when the order was created.

shippingMethod.description: The selected shipping method for the order.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents an item within an order.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product in the order.

unitPrice.description: The price of a single unit of the product at the time of the order.

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents an item in the user's shopping cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the cart item entity.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product in the cart.

definition.description: Stores product information. Each document represents a product in the catalog.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product.

definition.description: Stores the user's shopping cart items.  Authorization is based on the userId. Each document represents a product in the user's cart.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: cartItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the cart item.

definition.description: Stores order information. Each document represents an order placed by a user.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the Tulunadu E-Store application, focusing on product catalog management, shopping cart functionality, and order processing. Key considerations include authorization independence, data clarity, and predictable schema. Products are stored in a top-level `products` collection. User-specific shopping carts are managed in the `users/{userId}/cart` collection to ensure proper isolation and ownership. Orders will be managed using a top level `/orders` collection that relies on server-side validation to ensure data integrity.

*   **Authorization Independence:** The `users/{userId}/cart` collection ensures authorization independence. Security rules can be applied directly to this collection based on `request.auth.uid` without needing to `get()` data from other collections.

*   **QAPs (Queries are not Filters):** Products are stored in a flat `products` collection. Listing products can be done securely using pagination and filtering based on product attributes (category, price range, etc.) within the security rules. User specific cart items are stored in `/users/{userId}/cart` ensuring that a user can only ever see their own cart items.

*   **Invariants:** The structure supports invariants such as ownership (cart items are owned by the user), timestamps (orders have a `createdAt` field managed server-side), and data integrity (server-side validation for orders and stock updates).
